name: remote_mcp_server_hosting_provider_benchmarker
friction: Developers choosing a hosting provider for their MCP server have no data on how response time varies by provider or by the geographic location of the caller.
input: none — this is an always-on pipeline. It runs continuously without manual input.
output: A public website that shows, for each MCP server hosting provider, the measured response time (minimum, P50, P95, P99, maximum) broken down by the geographic location of the pinger that made the call. URL: https://mcp-server-hosting-providers-benchmark.github.io/tools_list_latency_publisher/

condition_1_pinger_geographic_coverage.description: Pingers (scripts that call each MCP server and record response time) are deployed in all major regions of the world.

type_a_near_end_users.description: Pingers placed near human users who run MCP clients locally on their device (desktop app, CLI). One pinger per major inhabited region.
type_a_near_end_users.rationale: When the MCP client runs on the user's device, the latency the user experiences depends on how far their device is from the MCP server.

type_b_near_llm_data_centers.description: Pingers placed near the data centers of the main LLM providers (e.g. OpenAI, Anthropic, Google). List of target LLMs to be established before placing these pingers.
type_b_near_llm_data_centers.rationale: When the MCP client runs server-side (e.g. ChatGPT iOS app, Claude web, API integrations), the latency depends on how far the LLM's server is from the MCP server — not the user's device location.

condition_2_hosting_provider_coverage.description: The list of MCP server hosting providers being measured covers the main options available to developers.

providers_not_yet_in_benchmark.name: Fly.io
providers_not_yet_in_benchmark.reason: Recommended by OpenAI in their official MCP server deployment guide at https://developers.openai.com/apps-sdk/deploy

providers_not_yet_in_benchmark.name: Google Cloud Run
providers_not_yet_in_benchmark.reason: Recommended by OpenAI in their official MCP server deployment guide at https://developers.openai.com/apps-sdk/deploy

providers_not_yet_in_benchmark.name: Azure Container Apps
providers_not_yet_in_benchmark.reason: Recommended by OpenAI in their official MCP server deployment guide at https://developers.openai.com/apps-sdk/deploy

providers_not_yet_in_benchmark.name: Replit
providers_not_yet_in_benchmark.reason: Code-hosting platform with always-on deployments, popular among developers

providers_not_yet_in_benchmark.name: Alpic
providers_not_yet_in_benchmark.reason: Maintains a ready-to-deploy Apps SDK starter for MCP servers, recommended by OpenAI in their official deployment guide at https://developers.openai.com/apps-sdk/deploy

condition_3_ping_frequency_validated.description: The interval between pings has been chosen deliberately, based on evidence — not left at the default 2-hour interval.
condition_3_ping_frequency_validated.question_to_answer: Does pinging every 2 hours keep the MCP server warm, so that measured latency is lower than what a real user would experience on a cold server?
condition_3_ping_frequency_validated.risk: If multiple pingers hit the same MCP server at the same time or within a short window, the server may stay warm between pings. The benchmark would then measure warm-start latency, not cold-start latency. Cold-start latency is what a real user experiences after a period of inactivity.
condition_3_ping_frequency_validated.done_when: A decision has been made on ping frequency — either: (a) the interval is long enough to guarantee cold starts (to be determined per hosting provider), or (b) the benchmark explicitly documents that it measures warm-start latency and explains why that is the chosen methodology.
condition_3_ping_frequency_validated.current_state: Interval is every 2 hours at even UTC hours (cron: 0 */2 * * *). All pingers are synchronized on the same UTC hour, which may amplify the warm-start effect.

when_to_use: Read this file when you need to understand what this benchmarker project produces, or to answer the question: 'Is the benchmarker complete?' — compare the current state of each condition in output_is_complete_when against reality.
when_not_to_use: To discover the individual pipeline components — read pipeline_components.json in this same repo instead.
